original_description: How to operate a Microsoft Fabric Data Agent in production: capacity and cost (the token-based "AI Query" billing model, monitoring via the Capacity Metrics app, baselines and alerting so usage doesn't run away), usage logging (the reality that Fabric has no native prompt log, the Microsoft Purview DSPM-for-AI path, and SDK-fronted logging), and lifecycle (Git serialization, CI/CD/ALM, and keeping the agent in sync with its semantic model so a model change triggers a re-test). Use this skill whenever someone asks how much a Fabric data agent costs, how to see what users are asking it, whether usage is logged, how to monitor capacity / set spend alerts, how to stop a data agent from blowing the capacity, how to version or deploy a data agent through dev/test/prod, or how to keep the agent and model in sync in Git. Trigger on "data agent cost / capacity / CU", "how much does the data agent use", "log what users ask the agent", "data agent usage / telemetry / monitoring", "Purview data agent", "deploy / promote / CI/CD the data agent", "git the data agent", "agent vs model out of sync". Companion to `fabric-data-agent` (design/build) and `fabric-data-agent-testing` (evaluation). Use even if the user only asks about one piece (e.g. just "can I see the prompts" or "how is it billed").

Use this skill whenever someone asks how much a Fabric data agent costs, how to see what users are asking it, whether usage is logged, how to monitor capacity / set spend alerts, how to stop a data agent from blowing the capacity, how to version or deploy a data agent through dev/test/prod, or how to keep the agent and model in sync in Git. Trigger on "data agent cost / capacity / CU", "how much does the data agent use", "log what users ask the agent", "data agent usage / telemetry / monitoring", "Purview data agent", "deploy / promote / CI/CD the data agent", "git the data agent", "agent vs model out of sync". Companion to `fabric-data-agent` (design/build) and `fabric-data-agent-testing` (evaluation). Use even if the user only asks about one piece (e.g. just "can I see the prompts" or "how is it billed").
final_description:
Use this skill for production and day-2 operations of an **existing Microsoft Fabric Data Agent** â€” not building or accuracy-testing it. Pick this skill when the user asks about:

- **Cost & sizing** â€” how much CU/capacity it consumes per question, token billing, total cost of ownership, sizing a rollout, whether F2/F4/F8 is enough, spend alerts.
- **Usage logging** â€” seeing what users ask it, prompt/audit logs, whether logging exists out of the box, Purview, telemetry.
- **Programmatic calls & auth** â€” invoking it from your own app or code, service principal vs. user login, SDK/MCP/Foundry.
- **Deployment & versioning** â€” Git, promoting devâ†’testâ†’prod, data-source bindings re-pointing, keeping it in sync with its semantic model (drift).

A Fabric data agent plus any one of these intents should trigger it.

history.description: Use this skill for questions about running a Microsoft Fabric **Data Agent** in production â€” anything past designing or testing it. It covers: what a data agent actually costs (per-question CU/token consumption, realistic TCO, whether F2/F4/F8 is enough, sizing a rollout); whether you can see what users ask it (native prompt logging vs. Purview DSPM, custom SDK logging); calling it from your own app or a service principal instead of a user login; monitoring capacity and setting spend alerts so it doesn't blow the budget; and lifecycle â€” versioning in Git, promoting devâ†’testâ†’prod, data-source bindings re-pointing, and keeping the agent in sync with its semantic model so model changes trigger a re-test. Trigger whenever a Fabric data agent is mentioned alongside cost, capacity/CU, billing, prompt/usage logging, auth/service principal, deployment/promotion, or model drift â€” even for just one of these. Companion to `fabric-data-agent` (build) and `fabric-data-agent-testing` (evaluate).

history.description:
Use this skill for operational, day-2 questions about running a Microsoft Fabric **Data Agent** in production â€” anything beyond building or testing the agent itself. Reach for it when the question involves:

- **Cost & sizing** â€” how much CU/capacity a data agent burns per question, total cost of ownership, whether F2/F4/F8 is enough, costing a rollout, spend alerts, runaway usage.
- **Usage visibility** â€” seeing or logging what users actually ask, prompt logging, Purview, audit, telemetry.
- **Invocation & auth** â€” calling the agent from your own app, service principal vs. user login, SDK/MCP/Foundry.
- **Lifecycle** â€” versioning in Git, promoting devâ†’testâ†’prod, data-source bindings re-pointing, deployment pipelines, keeping the agent in sync with its semantic model (drift).

Trigger if a Fabric data agent appears with any one of these themes. Companion to `fabric-data-agent` (build) and `fabric-data-agent-testing` (evaluate); route pure build or accuracy-evaluation questions there.

history.description:
Use this skill once a Microsoft Fabric **Data Agent** already exists and someone needs to run it in production. Trigger on questions about:

- **What it costs to run** â€” CU/capacity consumed per question, token billing, total cost of ownership, sizing a rollout, whether F2/F4/F8 is enough, spend alerts, runaway capacity.
- **Seeing what users ask it** â€” prompt/usage logging, "is there a log out of the box," audit, Purview, telemetry.
- **Calling it from code** â€” invoking the agent from your own app, service principal vs. user login, SDK/MCP/Foundry.
- **Shipping changes safely** â€” versioning in Git, promoting devâ†’testâ†’prod, data-source bindings re-pointing, deployment pipelines, keeping the agent in sync with its semantic model so changes don't silently drift.

Trigger whenever a Fabric data agent appears with cost, logging, auth/invocation, or deploy/version/drift intent â€” even just one. For designing or accuracy-testing the agent, defer to the build and testing skills.
